chief_complaint: A 38-year-old woman with a twin pregnancy presents with nausea, abdominal pain, tachycardia, hypertension, and disturbance of consciousness.
history: The patient was diagnosed with gestational hypertension and developed a thyroid storm during the peripartum period. She had no prior history of thyroid disease.
exam: Tachycardia, hypertension, disturbance of consciousness, and desaturation. Postoperative period findings included fever, diarrhea, and high-output heart failure with pulmonary edema.
labs: Diagnosis of thyroid storm confirmed using the Burch-Wartofsky point scale with 75 points.
